metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney is packed with family-friendly fun! I've found some fantastic free activities at museums and parks, as well as a colourful dance festival. Get ready for an exciting weekend!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Hands-on museum designed specifically for children, with collections of toys, design and childhood objects.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, daily activities, and space to play and explore. Perfect for sparking creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the daily activity schedule online before you go.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Large park with playgrounds, sand pits and paddling pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of space to run around and play, with different playground areas suitable for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle or take a bus from Hackney.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the lake.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life through a series of room displays and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: The front gardens and period rooms can be interesting for a 3-year-old, and the cafe is family-friendly.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Liverpool Street and get off near the Geffrye Museum.
transport.tip: Check for family events before you go.

activities.title: Spitalfields City Farm
activities.description: Community farm with a variety of animals, gardens and activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Kids can see and interact with farm animals, and there's space to play.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Shoreditch High Street, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check their website for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Safe and engaging space for toddlers to play.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or train to Stratford International, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Combine with a walk around the Olympic Park.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: Community farm with animals and gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: A chance to see farm animals up close and learn about where food comes from.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle or take a local bus.
transport.tip: Check for special events and activities on their website.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Great for interactive play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Embankment. Walk across the river.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Tiny Planet
activities.description: Theatre performance
activities.whyItWorks: Theatre performance for children

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shadwell then walk
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, fascinating exhibits, and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaur exhibits and interactive areas are captivating for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Piccadilly Line) directly to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits on space, technology, and science.
activities.whyItWorks: Pattern Pod is specifically designed for young children, and there are plenty of hands-on exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Piccadilly Line) to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Check the website for special events and activities.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore world history and culture through fascinating artifacts and exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Egyptian galleries with mummies and sphinxes are especially popular with children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube (Central Line) to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road station.
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack or Museum Explorer Trail from the Family Desk.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Indoor garden with panoramic views of London.
activities.whyItWorks: Unique experience with amazing views.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Monument
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven-acre park and playground with a small animal area, cafe, and paddling pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities for young children, including animals and play equipment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Russell Square or King's Cross.
transport.tip: Adults are only allowed entry if accompanied by a child.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Scenic escape with gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of activities, including gardens, play areas, and animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a bus to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check for events happening in the park.

activities.title: Holland Park
activities.description: Park featuring a playground with zip wire, swings, and see-saws, as well as woodland and peacocks.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of play equipment and natural attractions, suitable for different ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Holland Park station.
transport.tip: Explore the Kyoto Garden for a peaceful escape.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: A hub of arts, fun and food with kids' concerts, festivals, creative workshops, hands-on installations and space to scoot or picnic.
activities.whyItWorks: Welcoming, accessible and packed with energy

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Waterloo
transport.tip: Check the website for scheduled family activities.

activities.title: Fairy Tales
activities.description: Interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures, iconic characters and timeless tales.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and tactile elements make it engaging for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston.
transport.tip: Go early in the day for a calmer experience.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity that motivates kids to walk and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train to Waterloo, then another train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early in the day for a calmer experience.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines a zoo visit with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston. Then a bus to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Buy tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond
activities.description: A fun-filled family Easter adventure where 3-6s explore a series of installations to find the bees, build them a new hive and help get them there safely.
activities.whyItWorks: Good for the target age group.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: Children are invited to bring along a toy to lend for the performance and watch it come to life on stage, plus there's 10 minutes of playtime at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Great theatre experience

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to London Bridge
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Theatre production of Alice in Wonderland
activities.whyItWorks: Classic story brought to life on stage

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hammersmith
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Inside Aardman: Wallace & Gromit and Friends
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Showcases everything from idea development and storyboarding to model making and filming.
activities.whyItWorks: Wallace & Gromit are child friendly.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Mr Men Little Miss Takeover
activities.description: Step into the vibrant world of Mr Men/Little Miss through a series of interactive activities, including a treasure hunt
activities.whyItWorks: The event is free and offers interactive activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Battersea Power Station.
transport.tip: Check website for details
